question.text: An issuer plans a public issue of Rs. 3,500 crore. What SEBI filing fee must be paid?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For public issues exceeding Rs. 10 crore but up to Rs. 5,000 crore, the SEBI filing fee is 0.1% of the issue size. So 0.1% of Rs. 3,500 crore = Rs. 3.50 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 5 crore is the base fee for issues exceeding Rs. 5,000 crore (plus 0.025% of excess). Candidates may confuse the tier boundaries. Rs. 1 lakh is the flat fee for issues up to Rs. 10 crore. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fee calculations are specific to NISM Series IX. Other NISM exams focus on different fee structures (e.g., Series V-A covers mutual fund expense ratios). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always calculate the filing fee early in the mandate process — for large issues, this can be a material cost that needs to be budgeted and communicated to the issuer upfront.
